comment.body:
Regarding Russia and Ukraine, I think it’s important to understand the real
reasons behind all this. The goal is to build up European defense and move Russia away from China. Both would then act as counterweights to the Chinese and smooth out the balance of power, since the U.S. doesn’t have the capacity anymore to enforce a global peace. This is similar to Disraeli’s foreign policy. The goal overall is radically reshape the global system while preventing China from becoming dominant and preventing the decline of the U.S.

comment.body: And btw, Russia already had most of the land that it currently occupies in Ukraine from 2014. They’ve gained some, but most of it is third world type villages. The biggest goal was connecting the land bridge with to Crimea, which they accomplished.

comment.body:
VIX measures the IV of SPX options. So what is the difference? How could the IV of only calls be up (when IV affects calls and puts equally) and the VIX not be up? There also seems to be a conflation of trying to separate these two things, where one day VIX is a bad instrument that isn’t reflective of the IV of SPX options in this kind of context, then the next day Alma is using VIX in her Thursday analysis to state that volatility is sticky because it’s staying in the 26-27 range.

So which is it?

Please explain further you guys… and please explain what the VIX is if it doesn’t measure the IV of SPX options.

comment.body:
Spot up vol up in the context you’re using it in doesn’t seem to correlate to spot up VIX up, but spot up, IV of SPX options up (specifically calls). In negative MM GEX MM’s are buying the rally and selling the dump. 

If traders/customers are buying puts, who’s selling to them? If the MM’s/dealers are selling (shorting) the puts, they need to delta hedge by selling futures because that skews them to a long position, no? 

Not to mention the high negative gamma MM’s have from the JPM collar. Would that not start to play a pretty big roll the closer to expo that gets, adding to the negative gamma environment?


Volume today was pretty sparse, SPX volume was only this low on the way down since Feb 26th where we were also green and then continued down. If HF’s aren’t there like Alma said, that seems indicative to me of a still lack of liquidity. And a precarious place to be. 

Just thinking out loud, we’ll see what next week brings. Price action is doing exactly what I’ve wanted it to do this past week so the theory and thesis I have continues to be present. We shall see though as I have a peasants compared to what Alma is seeing and the info she can digest

comment.body:
What I mean is, when IV rises it does so for both calls and puts. It’s not like it is a massive variance where the VIX is down by 8% and calls will still be higher.

To figure out the 1 day move for VIX you’d divide it by 16. So the decay is still the same.

comment.body:
IV 30days out I believe, not IV right now. I’m not entirely sure IV impacts calls and puts equally, in fact I lean towards the notion that it doesn’t, which is why it’s useful to look at. Skew tells you what’s being bid and what the market is trying to price in. That’s why it’s useful. If IV is rising faster for calls vs puts or vice versa over fixed strikes over x amount of expirations, what would that tell you?

I’ve started to monitor VIX3M and VVIX a lot more now after the spaces with volsignals and AJ (The Architect). I may or may not post charts every so often of IV Term structure and my own IV skews I’ll be looking at as well. It’s just one piece of the puzzle. 

I’ll say this too because I see part of where the frustration is coming from, Alma is not a God. She’s a human just like everyone else. You need to take what you can from here and run with it in other ways outside of just this Substack. I’m taking everything I’m learning here, and taking stuff from VolSignals, the Architect, Convex Value, etc. and applying what I’m learning here to the concepts they’re teaching as well (not sharing her charts outside of here, did that on X once and she rebuked me lol).

 Alma is great, I’m never not going to say that, but I KNOW she didn’t learn everything she knows from one single person, it’s been a multitude of people over the years. You shouldn’t do the same either. 

And don’t give up. Persistence is what separates winners from quitters.
